response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is primarily a web and full-stack developer whose C++ exposure is limited to a single six-month role at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project. The position requires deep, current C++ expertise in networking, multi-threading, industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), and IEC 62443 secure development practices — none of which are evidenced in this profile. The candidate's career has been built around JavaScript/TypeScript, Node.js, and web-oriented event-driven architectures, which are substantially misaligned with the industrial automation and embedded systems focus of this role.
strengths: Experience building distributed, event-driven architectures and high-throughput microservices at Sociomantic
Familiarity with REST APIs and Node.js that partially overlaps with the IoT communication layer requirements
Demonstrated history of mentoring, knowledge-sharing workshops, and cross-functional collaboration
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over a decade old; C++ is absent from the candidate's own skills summary
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, or any relevant industrial protocol background
No cloud platform experience (Azure) and no embedded or IoT systems work relevant to the role's core requirements

key_factors.factor: No industrial or IoT domain experience
